name: node-randombytes
title: node-randombytes
summary: random bytes from browserify stand alone
description:
random bytes from browserify stand alone
randomBytes for the browser. Uses crypto/msCrypto.getRandomValues() in
the browser. In Node.js, it uses crypto.randomBytes().
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-randombytes because random bytes from browserify stand alone.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-randombytes as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-randombytes with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-randombytes --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
